questions.question: Explain the concept of operator precedence in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Operator precedence determines the order in which operators are evaluated in an expression
When multiple operators are present, higher precedence operators are evaluated first
Example: 2+3*4 is evaluated as 2+(3*4)=14, not (2+3)*4=20 because * has higher precedence than +

questions.question: List and explain the three types of operators that are RIGHT-to-left associative in Java.
keyPoints: This means expressions like a=b=c are evaluated as a=(b=c)

questions.question: What is the difference between postfix and prefix increment/decrement operators in Java?
keyPoints: Postfix (expr++) returns the value then increments
Prefix (++expr) increments then returns the value
Postfix has higher precedence than prefix
Example: int a=5; int b=a++; results in b=5, a=6

questions.question: Arrange the following operators in increasing order of precedence: &&, +, *, =, ==
keyPoints: + (addition) is additive operator with moderate precedence

questions.question: How does the logical AND (&&) operator differ from bitwise AND (&) in terms of precedence?
keyPoints: Bitwise AND (&) has higher precedence than logical AND (&&)
In expression a & b && c, evaluation is (a & b) && c
In expression a && b & c, evaluation is a && (b & c)
&& short-circuits (doesn't evaluate right if left is false) while & always evaluates both sides

questions.question: Explain the role of parentheses in overriding operator precedence with an example.
keyPoints: Parentheses have the highest precedence and force evaluation in specified order
Parentheses can make code more readable and override default precedence rules

questions.question: What will be the output of the following expression? int x = 10 - 5 - 2; Explain why.
keyPoints: Subtraction is left-to-right associative (same precedence operators evaluate left to right)

questions.question: Write a Java program to demonstrate the effect of operator precedence in a complex expression. Show how using parentheses changes the result.
keyPoints: Write a complete Java program with main method
Print both results to show difference
Explain the evaluation order based on precedence

questions.question: Explain all categories of operators in Java in decreasing order of precedence. Include associativity rules.
keyPoints: Most are left-to-right except unary, ternary, assignment which are right-to-left

questions.question: Evaluate the expression step by step: int result = 5 + 3 * 2 - 4 / 2 + (10 % 3); Show all intermediate steps and explain the precedence rules applied.
